danger_signals.visual_form: roadside barriers, walls, and environmental boundaries at circuit edges
danger_signals.meaning: collision hazard; crossing boundary results in crash/loss of control

danger_signals.visual_form: hairpin turns and sharp road geometry (especially in Acropolis circuit)
danger_signals.meaning: high-speed danger zone requiring precise steering; collision risk

danger_signals.visual_form: rival vehicles close to player car on track
danger_signals.meaning: risk of collision or being overtaken; competitive threat

safety_signals.visual_form: wide, straight racing lines with clear road ahead
safety_signals.meaning: safe acceleration zone; low collision risk

safety_signals.visual_form: amusement park structures (Big Forest), bridge infrastructure (Bay Bridge), architectural features (Acropolis)
safety_signals.meaning: visual waypoints and track identity; safe reference points for navigation

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: opponent cars rendered in distinct 3D polygon models and potentially different colors/designs
multiple opponent vehicles with distinct 3D geometry and visual differentiation from player car

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Big Forest circuit with amusement park theme and wider track sections
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: reduced opponent AI aggression and slower lap times; more forgiving track design

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bay Bridge circuit featuring the bridge structure as dominant environmental feature
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: moderate opponent difficulty; bridge crossing introduces elevation changes and technical driving requirement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Acropolis circuit with tight hairpin turns and complex road geometry
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: aggressive opponent AI; high precision required; multiple sharp turns create execution difficulty

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: opponent vehicles recede in distance on-screen
opponent vehicles visible in close proximity in rear-view perspective or side-by-side racing
transmission type options displayed; different car models shown in 3D
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player agency to customize vehicle handling characteristics before race

environmental_storytelling.example: Big Forest features amusement park structures (roller coaster, attractions) to establish leisure/family context
Bay Bridge circuit uses the iconic bridge structure itself as the primary visual and navigational landmark
Acropolis uses tight hairpin turns as visual manifestation of expert-level challenge; Beginner uses wider, more forgiving topology
Circuit environments rendered in 3D polygonal graphics to convey cutting-edge technology and sense of immersion

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: follows racing line; attempts to maintain or improve position relative to player; responds to player vehicle proximity
responds to steering input; accelerates/brakes based on player controls; collision results in loss of control/crash
supports four selectable viewpoints (V.R. View System) to accommodate different player preferences and driving styles

notes: Virtua Racing (Genesis 1994) is a racing simulation with minimal narrative or faction-based storytelling. World state is communicated entirely through track design, circuit difficulty presentation, and environmental theming. Danger is signaled through track geometry and boundary markers. No political or faction systems present. The game's core communication is through racing circuit identity and visual representation of difficulty progression (Beginner → Intermediate → Expert).
